name: Smart Path Finder
introduction: An interactive, high-performance pathfinding and routing engine implemented entirely from scratch in Python with zero external dependencies. The engine features custom-built data structures (binary min-heaps), dynamic obstacle avoidance, rush-hour traffic simulation (time-aware routing), and precomputed hub cache tables for optimal query speeds.
activities: Developed a dependency-free pathfinding and routing engine in Python, including a custom binary Min-Heap supporting O(log V) decrease-key operations.

Implemented Standard Dijkstra, Bidirectional Dijkstra (reduces search space by exploring outward from both source and target), and Time-Aware Dijkstra utilizing sinusoidal rush-hour traffic multipliers.
Designed a dynamic obstacle avoidance mechanism allowing real-time exclusion of nodes or edges without rebuilding the graph.
Engineered a Hub-Based Precomputation Cache that caches paths between high-degree structural nodes, scaling query times to sub-millisecond execution.
Created an interactive CLI and an empirical benchmarking suite (evaluation.py) to evaluate execution time, node exploration size, and cache hit metrics.
problemSolved: Routing engines often rely heavily on large third-party libraries and face scaling challenges when handling dynamic obstacles or hourly traffic shifts. Smart Path Finder solves this by building custom, optimized data structures (a binary min-heap), implementing bidirectional search to shrink the search space, and introducing a hub-based precomputation cache to speed up queries, all in a pure standard library Python implementation.
